reviews.review_text:
I tried playing this while i was drunk. turns out is easier to pilot DIY  Gundam fighter when you are half asleep and evade bulets the size of a Volvo XC40 than sober.

100/10, Would jagherbomb and play again
Great game tbh, shame I missed year 1 PvP. Classic FromSoft story, could use a few more missions ngl. Fun perfect too. 10/10 AC7 when

Finished the game four times already and did all three endings, so I can at least review the game as a whole.

This game offers so many customisation options unrivalled by other games, besides other AC games in the series from what I've heard. You will micromanage every small aspect of your AC because a singular part won't make that much of an impact, but all of the parts of your AC will have a drastic impact on the capabilities and playstyle of your mech making it unique.

This is also compounded by the fact that this game WANTS you to experiment. If you buy a part and you don't like it you can always sell it to get an 100% refund so you can try other parts out to slowly tweak your AC to your liking.

Replayability is also high due to the aforementioned three different endings this game has to offer in new game, new game plus, and new game plus plus.

Now as of the performance for me it runs smooth besides when some very intensive graphical effects take place which is quite rare. Having said that I am attaching my PC specs to the review to see that my computer is not just a mid range computer.

I would recommend this game to everyone. If you can spare the money to buy it full price do it, otherwise wait for a discount. Regardless of your financial situation I would have it on my radar and it would be a real shame to not buy this game.

Beautiful. This game is one of my all-time favorites. It’s incredibly well made, and the lore behind it is amazing too. Since I spent those 70 bucks on the game, I haven’t regretted a single penny. The world, the mechs, the animations, and the music are all just so good. I honestly can’t wait for Armored Core VII — if there’s ever going to be one.

My introduction to the series and now one of my favorite games ever. Fun combat, cool mech customization, and story that had me invested enough to get each ending and see the little differences in each interaction. For a game that I enjoyed enough to 100%, I'd absolutely recommend if you like action games or are a fan of mechs

Game is good up until the point you get to A - S rank in PVP. Then you start getting brutally molested by players who have more than 120 ping and use rocket launchers, dual - Zimms and kite. Besides that -- good game got me into AC4FA AC3LR and other entries.
To play this game you must armor your core and kill off other people all cause the lady and man in my ear told me to. Also screw you iguazu take a loss bro

Such a good game. As my first entry into the series, AC6 did a great job introducing me. Combat feels great and weighty, yet also gives you a sense of speed as you move around. The Story is great as well, and let me really get immersed into the world of Rubicon and the conflicts between its residents and the invading companies. The only real complaint I have is customization, as looking into past entries, I feel like there is a lack of parts, namely in the legs for your AC. Other than that, I'd say its more then worthwhile to play though at least once. I hope that we see a DLC or something of the sort like 4 Answer, but I wouldn't hold my breath.

This game was made for a very specific crowd of people but for the people who waited nearly a decade for the sequel to one of their favorite franchises, it was exactly what we needed. The gratifying combat of other FromSoft titles is cranked up to ludicrous levels of speed with plenty of options for customizing while easing you into a comfortable level of mastery that makes three playthroughs of the game with different endings feel intuitive and earned. The story is compelling but not especially complicated or important if you don't want it to be. The characters are generic but play their roles well and supplement the amazing gameplay with the proper environment to shine. The campaign and main game is fantastic and that alone makes this game a must play for any mecha or action game fans in general looking for a challenge. 

The PvP is also fantastic and I spent a lot of hours in it. The lack of real social features besides a few custom lobbies and prewritten greetings makes it nearly impossible to organize larger matches or gather the community in a meaningful way in game but the community is fantastic and the 1v1 matches are always competitive in the sense that every player you queue against is going to be trying their best to win and using their best equipment to do it. Wait times can vary but you can be sure to find a few matches against experienced players. 

Overall, it's an easy recommendation and one of my favorite games in the past decade, even if I never play it again. Looking forward to the obligatory DLC redux they're "not making".
One of the most perfect games out there, genuinely no comment and getting the 100% on this was some of the most challenging fun i've had. 11/10 would always recommend!!

Not a fan of mech games, mechs or mech anime. Despite this, the only issue with this game is there isn't Armored Core 7 for me to play after beating AC 6. 
All jokes aside, great game! Great story. Multiple builds and multiple endings so there is some great replay ability. 
Some of the boss battels are hard so there is that die, try again, die try again, die try again.... (rage), win. 
Worth the buy.

An absolute gem of a game. The game play is awesome and you can customize your AC to suit your own play style. The customization outside of the ac parts is also great and you can practically create any kind of AC you want. Combat is fast and responsive. The enemies are enjoyable to fight as well as some bosses. There's no padding or wasting you time when it comes to the story and game play. The characters are amazing and interesting. The story is  great, straight forward, and even hopeful and not just vague item descriptions with a few depression sessions like usual Fromsoft games. The only negatives i can think of are the stagger system making you build around building up stagger damage thus limiting your ability to make an original build. The second negative is that some bosses are just awful to fight against. Overall this game is an amazing mech game worth playing.

Looks complex and complicated at first.

But once you get its mechanics and combat system
you are in for one of the best adrenaline ride!

At this point, I think Fromsoft is the only company that could give you
'that' gaming experience.

The second most fun game I've played in the franchise behind AC4A, which is a VERY high regard from someone as blatantly biased to 4th gen entries as me.

Has a wonderful singleplayer campaign that doesnt overstay its welcome, and has a surprising level of replay value.

I'm gay for V.IV Rusty

this is one of the best modern mech games of this decade a genre that has been dead until this game came out this game mech customization is amazing you can do so many builds in this game it's crazy and story is actually really damn good as well and the combat is 10/10 i have no complaints about it if you like high octane action games or looking a good mech game to get into this is the game to play 10/10 would commit more war crimes again

Frantic combat, in-depth customisation, unsettling OST.  Good to see that Fromsoft can push themselves into new kinds of games and still come through with a polished and well-designed experience. Highly recommended.

There's been games that are hard and called soulslikes but what a lot of people forget is Fromsoft also made itself known in the mecha genre with the Armoured Core series and it comes as no surprise that the newest one blends what they've learnt over the years into a dangerously addictive dance of death where your slightest mistake leaves you wide open to incoming attacks and the fast paced aerial combat opens up new avenues of attack and things to watch out for.
its a very graphic intense game which is able to bring out the movement and color you don't normally see in a video game
Good game. Very replayable. Many unique builds.

Hot DAMN they weren't lying when they said this was the best fromsoft title.
It was genuinely one of the most fun experiences i've had in a while and i am sad that i waited this long to finally get the game. The gameplay is amazing, the story genuinely captured me (i did all 3 endings) and the MUSIC. Nggghhh... dont even get me started. The drops of the sickest ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ themes in the entire game on some of these bossfights made them hit harder than any dark souls boss.

When I played the game at launch, it didn't click with me. But I was really attracted to the world, music, and lore. The characters are well written and the art direction is also really good, so I finally gave it a second try and ended up finishing it. At first, it can feel tough, but once you learn how to control everything, it just becomes insanely fun. Even when you are killing minor enemies, it just feels so sick.

This game is so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ cool. 100/10

Armored Core VI is a good ride from start to finish if you have the reflexes and control dexterity to enjoy it.
few things are as pleasant as getting a cool and mechanically interesting boss fight to pay off all the grunt work you've been doing, add on the extra challenges heaped on you by NG+ and NG++ to test yourself and feel pride at winning.

The story is better than many fromsoft games and the trailer helped inspire some modern literature.
TL;DC
The game is good, the story works, the combat is challenging but rewarding.  Try it!
From Software title so expect a challenge.  Persevere, experiment with AC designs, hone your skills in AC testing, and stay calm.  Rinse, repeat.

This game rocks overall, but has a few too many gank fights for my liking.
Still, you can tell by my playtime that wasn't too much of an issue.

A buddy of mine recommended the game to me and I will admit its my first Armored Core and never even heard of the game but it is very addicting and I cannot wait for them to come out with more. If you love to take your time with creating a build to beat different enemies and people online, then this is the game for you.

An awesome experience, I missed the multiplayer part, and it took me 67 hours to 100% the game in terms of achievements. The mechanics are awesome and take understanding to learn, but aren't all hidden away behind community findings only.

Strong recommend for a great time with the game, it can get hectic, but you kinda need to look past it to get it.

Big ol mechs surviving this red stuff that takes your sense of agency, your sense of self,. Then, you're bossed around as a reward until some days you're told you're doing a good job, until you you get told you're doing a bad job. It's just like marriage.

Big Robots vs Bigger Robots go boom. Plus auto locking Missile launches! Meelee Energy weapons......and tough bosses with enough customisation options to keep busy till Armoured Core 7 comes out. So yeah its pretty much the best best mech game out there!!!

Imagine yourself in a world where people exist for the sole purpose of making you feel small, worthless and insignificant... Wait never mind that is the real world... Well anyway... this game is exactly that, but that's the charm of Fromsoft games. A weakling, worthless scrap that has been scooped out of the grave to fight. But you grow to increase your power and learn skills to beat down all the egotistical narcissists. The customization is beautiful, the weapons are fun, and the movement is just insane. Despite what some people may say, this is definitely one of the better mech games out there, and the story is just heartbreakingly beautiful. 10/10 would kiss Ayre if she were real, but the real waifu isn't her nor Rusty... its Walter. #justiceforwalter

Easily FromSoftware's best title if not tied up with Sekiro or ER. Fires of Rubicon is a great point for newcomers to jump in with as the game's core narrative and lore maintains itself as a solo story, and the combat and customization really bring out the ace pilot in a dedicated player. Replayability is fair, considering 3 different endings all achievable through NG+ through NG++.  Level design and graphics are mindblowing with all the detail and scale of each location and the soundtrack is absolutely tear-jerking. I recommend this game AND this series to any fan of FromSoftware's other works, fast paced piloting games, and to any fan of Mecha franchises like Armored Trooper VOTOMS and Mobile Suit Gundam. 10/10 better than Elden Ring

Gameplay is fun, especially in the later game, when you have access to most of the build options. 
Story is good, following a mercenary in a resource war between corporations that use mechs to fight each other.

I totally recommend this game; it's fast-paced. I loved the story; the VA is amazing, and despite that you never see what the characters look like (you interact only with their robots (the Armored Cores)), you get to love and hate many of them. The graphics are not as impressive at first, but they still look good. Also, the customization is great, as are the controls and music. If there's any downside It would be the PVP, which, at least on my few attempts, seems pretty much dead. I don't care much about that, but take that into consideration if it's important to you.
Great gameplay, story, and replayablility. under the radar fantastic FromSoft game.
Fast paced, fun, and with enough depth of skill and strategy to be very rewarding and enjoyable.
So its really good. Got a great story and awesome gameplay, but no coop. i mean there is a mod for that but those are spotty at best. I'd say wait for a sale and pick it up.

A very very good game. As a dev and a mech lover myself, I can say that it's worth every penny. There are even very good co-op community mods now, so the game can easily be enjoyed with friends as well.
A great, engaging and challenging game. While it's made by FromSoft, don't mistake it for a Soulsborne experience, but instead a look into one of their older franchises

Super great game! Always loved the Armored core series since back in the day on ps2 and was super excited when this game was announced played through a few times for different outcomes in the stories and for different optional bosses would definitely pick it up if you like mech fighting games!

Combat is fun as heck.
Story is intriguing.
Mech assembly is addicting.
Runs incredibly smoothly, haven't faced any stutters, crashes or frame drops.

Probably the best mecha game on PC right now. The story is engaging, the action addictive, and the thrill of beating every boss is sheer joy. Nothing gives me goosebumps more than the good ending for this game. 

Runs really well on my pc, but thats to be expected with my specs.
It eats your weapon swap inputs way too ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ often and a few bosses are kinda munted but the entire rest of the game makes up for it like hell

Graphics - 10
Art and Visuals - 10
Gameplay - 10
Story - Good but storytelling lacking
Overall - 9.9/10

I need more!!!

Pro's
Extensive Mech Customization
Fluid Control Layout
Stellar Visual Vibe
Re-playable Missions
Loadout Choice Dictates Success
Generous checkpoints and mid-mission assembly options let players change gear right after dying

Subjective Con's
Bland Story Delivery
Amazing game, I Am currently on my second playthrough and I have had a lot of fun playing it!
this game is epic. there is little else to say, other than well done from software. well freaking done!

this is one of the best games i've ever played. i tried dark souls and it was way too difficult, elden ring was better but i got burnt out by the open world, but this game has the PERFECT difficulty. i did my first full playthrough on the original, pre nerf patch, where a certain boss was... apparently difficult enough to get nerfed? i dont really understand why, because when i got to that part, while it was difficult, finally beating that boss basically tripled my skill level to the point i was able to have a very fun time afterwards, even with some of the bs this game throws at you. i will never forgive them for somehow adding bonewheel style enemies to a mech game. but the customization is incredible, and the game and story alone are good enough that i did three playthroughs. this is the only fromsoft game i've beaten, and one of very few games i've cared enough for to 100%. this is the first time i'm writing a game review, and it is genuinly impressive that i have only found a single complaint with this game in the many hours i've played. hell, every character is interesting, i never hated any of them. even snail, because even the biggest jerks feel human. and there's a lot of cool interactions like how you can take previous missions, but on the opposing side in ng+ playthroughs, and theres a secret interaction in one mission where youre supposed to kill a guy, and he runs away a ton, but if you dont attack him he'll pay you to let him live, and your actual pay for that mission is docked. incredible game, 10/10, please make more FromSoft, i'm begging you.
A surprisingly sophisticated GUNPLA model maker with a fun mech-fighter game mode!

Excellent game. Great combat, cool mech designs, good missions, great story.
I'm on my fifth? sixth? playthrough right now and reset my progress to play from scratch again. highly recommended

never before has a game left me wanting more of it this much. the weapons can feel a bit same-y with a good chunk just being stat increases on the prior iteration, but the way they all meld together feels incredible to play with
my favorite game, the mechanics are incredible, the world is insane in a good way and the story is good too but most importantly it gives a sense of scale few games can.

I need more Armored Core! This game is sooooo underrated. The game play is SOLID. The game loop addictive and rewarding. The narrative style is so creative. How did I feel so connected to characters I never even saw??

AC6 generally nails what makes mecha games fun. Customize your mecha, shoot other mechas, shoot big ol' machines and structures with your mecha.

Story itself is pretty good. Touching, but nothing crazy.

Gameplay itself is usually very fun. There are a few bad missions, but the vast majority of the game is well balanced, high-action mecha combat.

Overall, good time and I'm glad I bought it. One of a few games I have 100% completion on.

i will say after playing through all of the ac games this was alot of fun but i have a huuuuuge issue with the game forcing me to give it a 7 out of 10 sadly and thats the bosses some of the bosses really force you to play and build one way :c and thats really sad to me other than that amazing game multiple endings and some odd stuff to keep you playing and go "wuts happeneing?" lol i really loved it a whole lot and this game tugged my heart quite a bit! its just a shame i cant play the game the way i wish to :/

I bought this game because it was made by Fromsoft, without much certainty as to how much I'd like the game.  While I did have some limited knowledge of Armored Core as a franchise, this was the first and, so far, only AC game I've played.  

While I did enjoy the game a fair bit, I still haven't beaten it and don't really expect to ever.  I don't consider that a fault of the game in any way; I just allowed myself to get extremely burnt out.  After every completed mission I'd replay it to get an S rank.  Between trying to S rank each mission and looking for collectibles, I just lost all desire to continue.  I've tried to get back into the game several times but I still just lack any motivation to continue.  Nevertheless, this is a good game, well worth playing.  Also, I still go back to watch the reveal trailer for this game, including while I was writing this review.  It gives me chills every time I watch it.  Overall, I'd give AC 6 an 8/10.
the combat is truly very indepth and feels great when you get used to it, and getting used to it isnt hard at all aswell the graphics (imo) look good and isnt performance heavy
liked it but i didnt like the nerfing of my scores on the levels after also "balancing" some of the loadouts
Played this game since release, I'm still not over it. Best lobotomy I ever had 10/10 will spread Armored Core propaganda to my friends until I die.

genuinely one of the most beautiful games I've ever experienced, and easily the best mech game I've ever played (titanfall 2 close second)

Also, co-op mod goes hard

Just finished 100% on it, and I absolutely cannot recomend it enough!
The combat feels so smooth and responsive (unless you're doing multiplayer lol), the garage and customisation is top-tier, and it's definitely sitting at one of my favourite games to date. Only aspects that can be annoying is having to change your build all the time, but honestly that's part of the fun as well xD

Well worth your time and money, and definitely worth the effort if you want to finish it all off!

Armored Core 6 is a breath of fresh air in an industry filled with live services and countless DLC packs. With Fires of Rubicon, you get the whole package without any additional transactions, to "farm" is synonymous with just playing the game and earning its currency.

While it isn't necessarily the best entry in the series, it is perhaps the most approachable to newcomers, being a soft-reboot that doesn't need a PhD in previous AC lore to enjoy the story and many memorable characters in its singleplayer campaign that is the highlight of the experience.

It does have a multiplayer mode, but it is somewhat more flawed. The way the network is handled makes certain weapons a bit inconsistent to dodge, and the meta that forms around these multiplayer games can be frustrating for someone just aiming to have fun rather than win.

But the underlying gameplay of AC6 is great, shining in the singleplayer campaign where you take on hordes of weaker MTs, fellow ACs, or massive bosses that test your skills and build in different ways. Each part you pick for your loadout matters, and the game encourages you to experiment until you find what works for any specific mission.

Overall, AC6 sets the standard for what a good mech game should be, and hopefully Fromsoft will revisit this franchise again and build upon the foundation Fires of Rubicon has laid down.

Disclaimer: I am not a mecha fan, but I like Dark Souls so I had to play this.
The customization part felt not balanced at all. I went through most of the game with a mobility build but there was a particular enemy that just felt too frustrating. So I switched to the most tanky build possible - turns out you can ignore all enemy mechanics and just win.
If you happen to like a specific playstyle, you're probably handicapping yourself.

Everything else is fantastic: looks pretty, runs well, story definitely feels like a fromsoft game, and Ayre is hot.
I ain't even got much to say about the game.  The gameplay is tight, and the mechs are dope.  Get you some of this ♥♥♥♥.

So nice you'll play it twice!
And thrice!
And four times
aaaaand five times




and eight hundred and fourty-nine times...
I like this game 10/10
Incredible game, devoured my soul for 45+ hours on the really hard bosses.
Man I just started and only a couple of hours in but this feels like the old days like when AC1/Phantasma/Master of Arena came out. Feels perfect. Exactly the same vibe everything is so on point.

Armored Core VI absolutely nails the feeling of being a ridiculously fast death machine with a garage full of weapons. The customization is addicting, and tweaking your mech between missions becomes half the fun. Boss fights can be brutal, but finally overcoming them feels incredible in classic FromSoftware fashion. It’s chaotic, stylish, and probably the coolest mechs have felt in years.

The boss fights make this game un-fun, and their overtly gamey design completely devalues the simulation aspects of mech piloting. I hope that in future AC games, From remembers that they can do something besides the Soulsborne formula.
Its not that good. Zone of the Enders 2 is still superior even if it is way older.

I'm 10 hours into this game, and I'll keep trying it, just because it was 60 bucks.  But, here are my thoughts so far: I am coming from my experiences with Chromehounds and with Armored Core 4, and all the Mechwarrior games (and MechCommander and Battletech), and Heavy Gear, and Steel Battalion (yeah I have that huge controller).

In other words, I LOVE MECH GAMES.  But we have a problem here... is this a "mech game"?

I know that might sound odd at first but here me out-- This doesn't feel like a mech game. This is a Soulslike game wearing the wallpaper and the skin of a mech game. 

It's 3rd person only; no cockpit view. There's no locational damage, you just have HIt Points. You "roll dodge" against the bosses telegraphed attacks. The weapons are all really close range (but enemies can snipe you from across the map).  You see where this is going, right? In this game, you mostly close to 200 to 300 meters of the enemy and just do whatever attacks you have; the weapons honestly don't feel that different, it's either "It shoots fast and does small damage" or "it shoots slow and does big damage".

All of the bosses so far, I've died a few times on every single one... unless you're some insane pro gamer, if you play this game normally, blind, and just go into it without having read guides or watched videos, you're gonna die. I hate that. It is a defensible design choice, but personally I hate it.

This isn't a "bad game", but I am reviewing it as a "mech game" and right now, based on my 10 hours, it's not a very good one. This is some kinda souls-like wearing mech wallpaper.  Purchase accordingly.

I actually really like this game, but, just like in dark souls 1, the later half of the game is a mess and becomes very frustrating because it funnels you into very specific builds, especially cheesy ones. to me at least its pretty grating. I don't want to have to rebuild my AC from the ground up just to not make a mission far more harder than it needs to be. This title has been more frustrating than any of the soulsbourne games to me because of this.
fast moving robots doing fast moving robot combat against fast moving robots
Beat this on my playstation on release, got to NG+. Felt the call to sortie and had to get this on Steam so i can earn that NG++.

Fast-paced Fights, Cool Mecha, Sentient Gasoline Waifu

10/10 would hump a barrel of space gasoline, again

Steel wings cut the sky
Missiles bloom on Rubicon
One more sortie, Chief
Amazing storyline thus far, pairs well with the Amazon Prime series Secret Level episode 8. Plays harder but more rewarding than AC3 and AC4.
The best Mech fighter I've ever played... can't say I've played very many but this is some next level awesome stuff right here

Amazing customization I've lost countless hours(apparently 116)just messing around with the mech loadouts and paint, the story is amazing some of the routes just make ya feel things. Lots of replayability value as you have to finish campaign at least 3 times to get all storys, first time through is kinda hard unless you just do meta stuff but that's lame, after that it's pretty easy. The atmosphere and graphics are amazing they just make the feeling of gliding around on huge mechs feel so good, it makes me keep coming back. Laser slicer makes me feel cool.
never really "got" the mech genre but fighting IBIS with a lightweight mech had me doing the Danny Devito "I get it now" gif the whole time
This game rocks, plus it has a surprisingly in depth new game + experience

As much as I want to recommend this game, the fact that it fundamental cheats, changing damage values on the fly, making it so that it is all but luck if you win a mission. I understand it is supposed to be challenging, but the cheating breaks the game. I do not have hours that I can waste trying to get lucky and advance through this broken game.

AC VI is an okay game - not terrible, not great. Most likely quite a few people will get a lot out of this, but I am coming from a different angle.

I fell in love with earlier ACs. This once was a genre of its own, a category where mainly only the earlier AC titles belonged. Highly frantic arcade like battles with a level of control that is only rivaled by the vast amount of possible customizations for your mechs. Insanely quick actions and enough energy to actually perform your crazy maneuvres. In other words: you were in control and the developers did not fear that at all.

But with this AC title all that belongs to the past. AC isn't terribly slow now, but I sense a nature of decision making that just doesn't go well with players becoming too strong, so you're kind of embedded in a clamped and more controllable environment, equipped with medikits. This just doesn't seem right to me. Obviously a crazy amount of effort ran into asset creation, world building, dialogues, effects and so on, but that all just makes it your boilerplate modern triple A kind of game. Among these, this is a pretty solid and enjoyable 'big game', but alas, it also ruined the series for me. Ultimately I didn't get what I was expecting from AC, didn't finish the main missions halfway into the game. I never got excited and the time spent so far did not at all feel rewarding. The mission briefings and handler dialogues felt really bland and worn out at some point, and the loading times ultimately gave me the rest.

I expect many other players would be pretty happy with such a title in their library, but I couldn't see what's in it for a seasoned AC player that replayed the 1on1 with White Glint from AC For Answer just for the heck of the insane difficulty and speed of battle. Winning this seemed only possible if you really became a good player - or at least they made me feel like it, which I'm cool with.

Will probably replay the older ACs at some point, but I'll pass on what AC has become.
Not fun. Mech game with obnoxious from soft boss fights where all that matters is having the right build not being good.
another piece if ♥♥♥♥ you need to pay full price and then tinker with to make it work, good game plagued by the same ♥♥♥♥♥♥ standard as all others
Dark souls with giant customizable mechs, and a dash of schizophrenia. 10/10
Truly different feeling to heavy western mechs. It's not an anime in the stroy but the mech actions will make you feel like one of those pilots - reccommend using a controller
Literal ♥♥♥♥ game and ♥♥♥♥ balance of weapons.

Overall this is an excellent experience.
However, the difficulty curve is all over the place.  There's no curve that prepares you over time for what is to come.  Instead, it's highly casual for hours and then... Bang! Here's a boss you're not prepared for.  Good luck.  To be fair most of them were a good balance of difficulty however...

There are two boss fights where From Software decided to shoehorn their "git gud" dumbass souls like mentality into them where you have to spend more time than I am willing just to figure out how to beat them.
You think I want to spend all day learning how to beat a boss?  Get real.  I have other games to play, and other ways I like to spend my time.  Your game isn't THAT good that I want to spend so much time on it.
Basically Elden Ring but with customizable robots

The game is only allowing me to play online multiplayer matches.  I guess its pack to my ps3 to play armored core missions, lame.  too expensive of game to not have campaigns.  Combined with taking over an hour to load a match.  Total disappointment!
As an arsonist, I really like this game, I mean all I just saw is "fires of rubicon" and was like hell yeah now thats my kind of game. Gameplay also very good.
100% on PS5. Will do it again on PC. Why does 32:9 ultrawide only work in Fullscreen mode? It's very unnecessary. Please allow 32:9 for Borderless Windowed mode.
Super fun game. Don't need to be a fan of the mech genre to enjoy
YOU CAN REPEAT MISSIONS FOR MORE MONEY, THE GAME JUST NEVER TELLS YOU.

the coral whispered to me...and it said...

"♥♥♥♥ it, we boost"
Fun and fast-paced mech game. Some of the bosses can be tough but only one of them was outright BS; the rest are cool and were fun to fight.
cool robots, controls better than dark souls
From Software is literally incapable of missing.
Brilliant fast paced mech action, loads of customization, well delivered plot. A must play!
This is my favorite game ever and I love it and I want Rusty
Rubicon has fallen, Billions must burn
